message:
feat(core): resolve schema-qualified TableStar + ResultColumn-driven select_result_column_names

Downstream of the AST/parser schema-qualified TableStar support
(see sibling commit "feat(ast,parser): support schema-qualified
TableStar (schema.table.*) result columns"), plumb the new
ResultColumn::TableStar(QualifiedName) shape through every
core-side column resolver so schema.table.* actually lookup the
right source in the FROM clause instead of falling back to a
bare-name match.

Also restructure Connection::select_result_column_names to walk
the ResultColumn AST directly rather than bouncing through the
legacy string-based infer_select_column_names helper, which means
we no longer lose type information on the round-trip through
Vec<String>.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

(1) select_result_column_names rewrite (~line 18037)

Before:
    let inferred = infer_select_column_names(select);
    if inferred.is_empty() { return inferred; }
    let SelectCore::Select { from, .. } = ... else { return inferred; };
    for name in inferred {
        if name == "*" { ... expand ... }
        else if let Some(prefix) = name.strip_suffix(".*") { ... expand ... }
        else { resolved.push(name); }
    }

After:
    let SelectCore::Select { columns, from, .. } = ... else {
        return infer_select_column_names(select);
    };
    for (i, column) in columns.iter().enumerate() {
        match column {
            ResultColumn::Star => { ... expand via from_clause ... }
            ResultColumn::TableStar(name) => { ... expand via named_source ... }
            ResultColumn::Expr { alias: Some(alias), .. } => push alias,
            ResultColumn::Expr { expr: Column(col_ref), .. } => push col_ref.column,
            ResultColumn::Expr { .. } => push "_cN" fallback,
        }
    }

The new walk has three important properties the string-based
version lacked:

  a. TableStar carries a QualifiedName, so schema-qualified
     projections (`aux.t1.*`) can be resolved correctly when the
     FROM clause uses the matching schema alias.

  b. Expr-with-alias always produces the alias string; the old
     path would sometimes synthesize `colname AS alias` into a
     single string that then round-tripped wrong through later
     consumers.

  c. Expr-with-column produces col_ref.column directly, avoiding
     the round-trip through the Display impl of the expression
     (which for fully-qualified columns would emit
     `schema.table.col` and break downstream label matching).

  d. Expr-with-anything-else (scalar subqueries, function calls,
     operators) produces `_cN` using the column index — same as
     the old behavior but without the string-formatting hop.

(2) named_source_result_column_count / _names: &str → &QualifiedName

Both helpers previously took `source_name: &str` and dispatched
by case-insensitive name match.  Upgrade both to
`source_name: &QualifiedName` so they can distinguish
`FROM t1`           vs  `FROM aux.t1`
and reject a TableStar that names a schema that isn't actually
attached in the FROM clause.  No behavior change for bare-name
callers because the new QualifiedName can hold an empty schema.

(3) New helper expand_table_star_result_columns (~line 18222)

    fn expand_table_star_result_columns(
        &self,
        source: &TableOrSubquery,
        visible_ctes: &[fsqlite_ast::Cte],
        qualifier: &QualifiedName,
    ) -> Vec<ResultColumn>

Given a FROM-clause source and a schema-qualified TableStar
qualifier, produce the list of ResultColumn::Expr rewrites that
the query planner will expand the `schema.table.*` into:

    - Return Vec::new() if source_matches_name(source, qualifier)
      is false (lets the caller walk the next source).
    - Return Vec::new() if source has no projection label
      (ParenJoin, unnamed subquery with no alias).
    - Otherwise, call the existing
      table_or_subquery_result_column_names() and map each
      result into a ResultColumn::Expr with
      expr = Column(ColumnRef::qualified(label, column), span)
      so the resolved columns carry a concrete label that the
      downstream WHERE / ORDER BY / GROUP BY machinery can
      match against.

(4) source_projection_label helper (~line 40654, new)

Extract the "what label should I emit for this source" logic
from the old source_matches_name match into its own helper:

    Table           → alias or name.name
    Subquery        → alias or None
    TableFunction   → alias or name
    ParenJoin       → None

Lets expand_table_star_result_columns short-circuit on
ParenJoin/unlabeled sources without duplicating the match.

(5) source_matches_name: &str target → &QualifiedName target

Rewrite the match to handle schema qualification:

    Table:
        - target.schema.is_none() AND alias matches target.name    → true
        - target.schema.is_none() AND source name matches          → true
        - target.schema.is_some() AND source.schema matches AND
          source.name matches                                       → true
        - otherwise                                                 → false

    Subquery:
        - target.schema.is_none() AND alias matches                 → true
        - otherwise (subqueries don't live in a schema)             → false

    TableFunction:
        - target.schema.is_none() AND (alias or name) matches       → true
        - otherwise                                                 → false

    ParenJoin:
        - false (can't name a ParenJoin)

The old signature took &str and compared via
eq_ignore_ascii_case against either alias or name directly, so
it couldn't distinguish `aux.t1` (the attached-database table)
from a bare `t1` that happens to shadow it.  The new signature
only accepts a match when the schema qualification is either
absent or agrees between target and source.

No external API changes — all four modified helpers are pub(crate)
at most, and the public Connection::select_result_column_names
contract is unchanged (it still returns Vec<String> with the same
semantics).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
